steps.title: Identify what low diversity means for trade
steps.content: The passage explains that marketplaces form because people want to trade for resources they don't have. If scholars underestimated ecological diversity, they'd wrongly assume that different regions all produced similar resources — meaning there'd be nothing different to trade for.

steps.title: Match the assumption to the blank
steps.content:
Choice C directly states the assumption they'd make: marketplaces wouldn't enable people to acquire many products different from what they already produce. The other three either shift focus to external trade (A), contradict themselves (B), or address production surplus rather than product variety (D).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
